notes: Curated unique per-spoke blog topics for the spoke blog network (Phase 2). Each brief is operator-pinned: it bypasses SERP profiling, is pinned by the decision-router, and renders ONLY on its target spoke domain (target_site -> frontmatter.domains) with a self-canonical spoke URL and exactly one contextual, branded-local in-body link back to the most-relevant hub city/service page. Topics are deduped against the live blog corpus (network-wide novelty gate) and against each other — each is a distinct pest family with a real Sarasota-local hook. Anchors follow content-ops/anchor-and-content-playbook.md (branded-local-heavy). Hub links MUST be absolute https://www.wavespestcontrol.com/... never {{siteUrl}}. Never hardcode Waves pricing — link to /pest-control-calculator/.

briefs.working_title: Bed Bugs and Sarasota Vacation Rentals: Siesta Key & Lido Key Short-Term-Stay Risk
secondary_kws: how to check a rental for bed bugs
briefs.thesis: Siesta Key and Lido Key run on heavy short-term-rental turnover, and bed bugs are a hitchhiker pest that rides in on luggage from the last guest. Explain why beach-rental density raises the risk, how a host or traveler inspects a unit, and why DIY sprays drive an infestation deeper instead of ending it.
outline: Why Sarasota's barrier-island rentals (Siesta Key, Lido Key) see more bed-bug introductions: constant guest turnover + luggage from out of state
How bed bugs actually spread between rental units and laundry — not a cleanliness problem
A practical 5-minute inspection a host or guest can do (mattress seams, headboard, box-spring corners, luggage rack)
Why store-bought foggers and sprays scatter them and miss the harborage
What a professional bed-bug treatment looks like and why early detection in a rental matters for reviews and bookings
verify_notes: Keep all guidance non-alarmist and accurate to bed-bug biology; no pricing claims in the body.

briefs.working_title: Carpenter Ants in Sarasota's Coastal Live-Oak Neighborhoods (West of Trail, Laurel Park)
briefs.intent: Sarasota homeowner in a mature-canopy neighborhood, problem-aware
briefs.hub_anchor: the Sarasota pest control team at Waves
briefs.thesis: Sarasota's older West-of-Trail and Laurel Park neighborhoods have a mature live-oak canopy, and those oaks (plus moisture-damaged wood) are exactly what large black carpenter ants nest in before they move into a house. Make this post Sarasota-specific to the coastal-oak neighborhoods — do NOT write a generic carpenter-ant article — and clearly distinguish carpenter-ant galleries from termite damage.
outline: Why Sarasota's coastal live-oak neighborhoods (West of Trail, Laurel Park) are carpenter-ant country: mature oaks, moisture, and old-growth landscaping
How to tell carpenter ants from other big ants — and from termites (frass vs mud tubes, smooth galleries vs soil-packed)
The moisture connection: roof leaks, irrigation overspray, and tree limbs touching the roofline
Why they keep coming back after spray-and-pray — you have to find the parent nest
What a Sarasota carpenter-ant inspection and treatment actually targets
FAQ: 5 common Sarasota carpenter-ant questions
verify_notes: Stay Sarasota-coastal-oak specific so this clears the novelty gate against the generic carpenter-ant post already live; carpenter ants do not eat wood (they excavate) — be accurate.

briefs.working_title: Drain Flies in Sarasota Bathrooms and Kitchen Sinks: Why They Won't Go Away
secondary_kws: how to get rid of drain flies
briefs.thesis: Drain flies breed in the organic gunk lining slow or seldom-used drains, and Sarasota's humidity plus the older-home plumbing common across the city keeps that film wet year-round. Explain where they actually breed, why surface sprays never fix it, and how to break the breeding cycle.
outline: What drain flies are and how to confirm them (the tape-over-the-drain test) — small fuzzy 'moth' flies, not fruit flies
Why Sarasota homes see them year-round: humidity plus older-home and guest-bath plumbing that sits unused
Where they really breed — the biofilm inside the drain, not the room — so room sprays do nothing
How to break the cycle: mechanical cleaning of the drain wall + enzyme treatment, and finding the right drain
When recurring drain flies signal a bigger plumbing or sewer issue worth a professional look
FAQ: 5 common Sarasota drain-fly questions
verify_notes: Drain flies (Psychodidae) breed in drain biofilm — keep the biology accurate; no pricing in the body.

briefs.working_title: Springtails Swarming Your Sarasota Lanai and Pool Deck After Summer Rain
secondary_kws: what are the tiny bugs on my pool screen
briefs.intent: Sarasota homeowner with a screened lanai/pool, rainy-season problem-aware
briefs.thesis: After Sarasota's summer afternoon storms, tiny jumping springtails erupt across screened lanais and pool decks because saturated soil and mulch push them toward dry surfaces. Explain the rain-and-moisture trigger specific to SWFL screened-lanai living and why they're harmless but fixable.
outline: What springtails are and why they suddenly 'swarm' a Sarasota lanai after rain (they jump, they're not fleas, they're harmless)
The rainy-season moisture trigger: saturated mulch, overwatered beds, and afternoon storms driving them onto pool decks and screens
Why screened-lanai living in SWFL concentrates them at the door tracks and deck edges
Moisture corrections that actually reduce them: mulch depth, irrigation timing, drainage away from the lanai
Why a perimeter treatment plus moisture fixes beats hosing them off every afternoon
FAQ: 5 common Sarasota springtail questions
verify_notes: Springtails are harmless and moisture-driven — keep the framing reassuring and accurate; tie explicitly to Sarasota rainy season.

briefs.working_title: Why Fleas and Ticks Are a Year-Round Problem in Sarasota's Mild Coastal Climate
secondary_kws: yard flea and tick treatment Sarasota
briefs.thesis: Most of the country gets a winter freeze that resets the flea and tick population. Sarasota doesn't — the mild coastal climate plus barrier-island wildlife means fleas and ticks breed all twelve months, so pet owners who treat seasonally fall behind. Explain the no-freeze reality and the yard-plus-pet approach that actually keeps up.
outline: Why there's no 'off season': Sarasota's lack of a hard winter freeze lets fleas and ticks breed year-round
The barrier-island wildlife factor — raccoons, opossums, and deer reseeding yards near the coast
Why treating only the pet (or only in summer) leaves the yard reservoir intact
The 90% you can't see: eggs, larvae, and pupae in the yard and home, and why timing matters
A year-round yard-and-perimeter plan for Sarasota pet owners — coordinate with your vet's on-pet product
FAQ: 5 common Sarasota flea-and-tick questions
verify_notes: Coordinate-with-your-vet framing for on-pet products; no medical or pricing claims in the body. Keep the no-winter-freeze point Sarasota-specific.
